---
title: "Indian Cupscale Grass (Sacciolepis indica)"
slug: "sacciolepis-indica"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474874490/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Indian Cupscale Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474874490/medium.jpg" alt="Indian Cupscale Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/ahevesh">
ahevesh</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264444394">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=168278)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare in Florida found in isolated sightings in wet, disturbed areas. 

## Natural history notes

A grass characterized by its upright stems and dense, spike-like seed heads. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as ditches, pond margins, and disturbed wetlands. It grows primarily during the warmer months and can form dense stands in suitable conditions.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/168278-Sacciolepis-indica)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/1503)
